articles.title: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can...” why purpose, not circumstances, often decides who keeps going when life becomes unexpectedly hard — learn the uplifting wisdom from the philosopher who challenged the world to think beyond comfort and hardship
articles.description: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.” We often wonder why some people break under pressure while others survive the hardest trials imaginable. The answer lies in a profound human truth about resilience and inner purpose. When you possess a powerful why to live for, your mind can endure any difficult how.

articles.title: 'Trump Only World Leader Sympathetic To You': JD Vance Rebukes Israeli Ministers Over Iran Deal
articles.description: JD Vance defended Donald Trump's diplomatic outreach to Tehran and suggested some Israeli officials have fundamentally misread their country's position.

articles.title: ‘NYT will endorse a dead armadillo over a Republican’: Kennedy rebukes coverage of Iran peace deal
articles.description: Sen. John Kennedy unleashed a fiery defense of President Trump's Iran peace deal, accusing critics and media outlets of unfairly attacking the agreement before seeing the final terms. Kennedy mocked coverage of the deal, joking that some newspapers would 'endorse a dead armadillo over a Republican,' while arguing that Iran's leaders cannot be trusted without strict enforcement measures. The Louisiana Republican also delivered a blistering assessment of Tehran, warning that the Iranian government has a long history of deception and insisting that any agreement must include tough verification mechanisms. His comments come as the Trump administration faces mounting questions over sanctions relief, oil exports, and Iran's nuclear commitments.

articles.title: Putin-Iran chopper deal with night vision tech stuns Trump amid peace deal? | Russia| US| Hormuz
articles.description: Just as President Trump thanks Vladimir Putin for Russia’s “neutrality” in the Iran war, Moscow quietly signs a major helicopter deal with Tehran—equipping Mojtaba’s forces with new, night‐vision‐capable aircraft. The move blindsides Washington and Israel, feeding a narrative that Putin has outmaneuvered Trump by talking peace while upgrading Iran’s capabilities behind his back.

articles.title: Who are Usain Bolt’s parents? Meet the couple who helped raise the fastest man alive
articles.description: Usain Bolt’s parents, Wellesley and Jennifer Bolt, were central figures in his journey from a small Jamaican village to global sporting greatness. Jennifer offered emotional support, while Wellesley provided discipline and guidance. They still live in Sherwood Content and run their local store, enjoying the rewards of the success their son worked so hard to achieve.

articles.title: 'TVK a democratic revolution’: After 55 years in CPI, veteran leader Mahendran joins Vijay’s party
articles.description: Veteran CPI leader C Mahendran has joined actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, citing a desire to strengthen communist ideology and public welfare. Mahendran, a seasoned politician with a five-decade record, believes TVK offers a platform for ordinary citizens and aims to eradicate corruption. He envisions the party as a mass movement, marking a significant shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: Pakistan PM's visit to Switzerland postponed due to electronic signing of US-Iran deal: Spokesman
articles.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has postponed a visit to Switzerland for a US-on Thursday. "The proposed visit has been postponed as the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has already been electronically signed, has entered into force, and is now under implementation," spokesman Mosharraf Zaidi said, adding Pakistan would support the next phase of several "technical-level" tracks. bur-ceg/lga

articles.title: Trump's Obama Rant Takes Wild Turn as He Drops Profanity During Iran Deal Remarks | WATCH
articles.description: President Donald Trump sparked fresh controversy at the G7 Summit in France after dropping a profanity while criticizing Barack Obama's Iran nuclear deal. During remarks about his proposed agreement with Tehran, Trump claimed Iran mocked Obama and defended his own approach to negotiations. The expletive-laced moment quickly went viral, overshadowing ongoing discussions surrounding the new U.S.-Iran deal and a reported $300 billion reconstruction proposal.

articles.description: A former Delhi Police Sub-Inspector, Nishant Tomar, has garnered attention for leaving his secure government job in India to relocate to Australia. He successfully built a new life from scratch, becoming a homeowner and a real estate agent in Adelaide, emphasizing that his journey was driven by taking chances and perseverance, not luck.

articles.title: Breaking: US-Iran MoU signed remotely by both Trump and Pezeshkian to end the brutal Middle East war
articles.description: A historic diplomatic breakthrough appears to have taken effect as the United States and Iran reportedly signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending months of conflict and opening a new phase of negotiations. According to multiple reports, U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed the agreement remotely, bringing the framework into force ahead of a planned diplomatic meeting. The deal is expected to focus on ending hostilities, restoring regional stability, reopening key shipping routes and launching further talks on Iran’s nuclear program, sanctions relief and broader security issues.
